<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Sharkpedia</title>
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
    <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400;0,700;1,400&amp;family=DM+Sans:wght@300;400;500&amp;display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
    <style>
      *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }

      :root {
        --ocean-deep: #03253b;
        --ocean-mid: #0a4a6e;
        --ocean-light: #1a7fa8;
        --ocean-pale: #c8e9f5;
        --foam: #f0f8fc;
        --sand: #f7f3ee;
        --ink: #0d1f2d;
        --muted: #4a6070;
        --accent: #e05c2e;
        --accent-light: #fdeee8;
      }

      html { scroll-behavior: smooth; }

      body {
        font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif;
        background: var(--sand);
        color: var(--ink);
        min-height: 100vh;
      }

      /* ── HEADER ── */
      header {
        background: var(--ocean-deep);
        color: white;
        padding: 0;
        position: relative;
        overflow: hidden;
      }

      .header-bg {
        position: absolute;
        inset: 0;
        background-image: url("data:image/jpeg;base64,");
        background-size: cover;
        background-position: center 40%;
      }
      .header-bg::after {
        content: '';
        position: absolute;
        inset: 0;
        background:
          linear-gradient(to right, rgba(3,37,59,0.85) 0%, rgba(3,37,59,0.5) 60%, rgba(3,37,59,0.3) 100%),
          linear-gradient(to top, rgba(3,37,59,0.6) 0%, transparent 50%);
      }

      .wave-line {
        position: absolute;
        bottom: 0;
        left: 0;
        right: 0;
        height: 60px;
      }

      .header-inner {
        position: relative;
        z-index: 1;
        max-width: 1100px;
        margin: 0 auto;
        padding: 80px 40px 100px;
      }

      .eyebrow {
        font-size: 11px;
        font-weight: 500;
        letter-spacing: 0.18em;
        text-transform: uppercase;
        color: var(--ocean-pale);
        margin-bottom: 16px;
      }

      h1 {
        font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
        font-size: clamp(52px, 8vw, 96px);
        font-weight: 700;
        line-height: 0.95;
        letter-spacing: -0.02em;
        margin-bottom: 28px;
      }

      h1 em {
        font-style: italic;
        color: var(--ocean-pale);
      }

      .header-desc {
        font-size: 17px;
        font-weight: 300;
        line-height: 1.7;
        color: rgba(255,255,255,0.75);
        max-width: 600px;
      }

      .stat-row {
        display: flex;
        gap: 40px;
        margin-top: 48px;
        padding-top: 32px;
        border-top: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12);
      }

      .stat { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; }
      .stat-num {
        font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
        font-size: 28px;
        font-weight: 700;
        color: white;
      }
      .stat-label {
        font-size: 12px;
        color: rgba(255,255,255,0.5);
        text-transform: uppercase;
        letter-spacing: 0.1em;
      }

      /* ── NAV ── */
      nav {
        background: white;
        border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.06);
        position: sticky;
        top: 0;
        z-index: 100;
        box-shadow: 0 1px 12px rgba(3,37,59,0.06);
      }

      .nav-inner {
        max-width: 1100px;
        margin: 0 auto;
        padding: 0 40px;
        display: flex;
        align-items: center;
        gap: 32px;
        overflow-x: auto;
        scrollbar-width: none;
      }

      .nav-logo {
        font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
        font-size: 15px;
        font-weight: 700;
        color: var(--ocean-deep);
        white-space: nowrap;
        padding: 16px 0;
        flex-shrink: 0;
        text-decoration: none;
      }

      .nav-divider { width: 1px; height: 20px; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.1); flex-shrink: 0; }

      .nav-links { display: flex; gap: 4px; }

      .nav-link {
        font-size: 13px;
        color: var(--muted);
        text-decoration: none;
        padding: 6px 12px;
        border-radius: 6px;
        white-space: nowrap;
        transition: background 0.15s, color 0.15s;
      }

      .nav-link:hover { background: var(--foam); color: var(--ocean-mid); }

      /* ── MAIN ── */
      main {
        max-width: 1100px;
        margin: 0 auto;
        padding: 0 40px 80px;
      }

      /* ── SECTION LABEL ── */
      .section-header {
        padding: 64px 0 32px;
        display: flex;
        align-items: baseline;
        gap: 16px;
      }

      .section-title {
        font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
        font-size: 13px;
        font-weight: 400;
        font-style: italic;
        color: var(--muted);
        letter-spacing: 0.05em;
      }

      .section-line { flex: 1; height: 1px; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.08); }

      /* ── SHARK CARDS ── */
      .shark-grid {
        display: grid;
        grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(320px, 1fr));
        gap: 2px;
        background: rgba(0,0,0,0.06);
        border: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.06);
        border-radius: 16px;
        overflow: hidden;
      }

      .shark-card {
        background: white;
        display: flex;
        flex-direction: column;
        transition: background 0.15s;
      }

      .shark-card:hover { background: var(--foam); }

      .card-image-wrap {
        aspect-ratio: 4/3;
        overflow: hidden;
        background: var(--ocean-deep);
        position: relative;
      }

      .card-image-wrap img {
        width: 100%;
        height: 100%;
        object-fit: cover;
        display: block;
        opacity: 0.9;
        transition: opacity 0.3s, transform 0.4s;
      }

      .shark-card:hover .card-image-wrap img {
        opacity: 1;
        transform: scale(1.03);
      }

      .img-placeholder {
        width: 100%;
        height: 100%;
        display: flex;
        align-items: center;
        justify-content: center;
        background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--ocean-deep), var(--ocean-mid));
        color: rgba(255,255,255,0.3);
        font-size: 48px;
      }

      .status-badge {
        position: absolute;
        top: 12px;
        right: 12px;
        font-size: 10px;
        font-weight: 500;
        letter-spacing: 0.08em;
        text-transform: uppercase;
        padding: 4px 10px;
        border-radius: 20px;
      }

      .status-cr { background: #fdeee8; color: #b53d1a; }
      .status-en { background: #fef3cc; color: #8a5c00; }
      .status-vu { background: #e8f5e9; color: #2a6b32; }

      .card-body { padding: 20px 22px 24px; flex: 1; }

      .card-tag {
        font-size: 10px;
        font-weight: 500;
        letter-spacing: 0.12em;
        text-transform: uppercase;
        color: var(--ocean-light);
        margin-bottom: 6px;
      }

      .card-name {
        font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
        font-size: 22px;
        font-weight: 700;
        color: var(--ink);
        line-height: 1.15;
        margin-bottom: 4px;
      }

      .card-latin {
        font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
        font-style: italic;
        font-size: 13px;
        color: var(--muted);
        margin-bottom: 14px;
      }

      .card-desc {
        font-size: 14px;
        line-height: 1.65;
        color: #3d5060;
      }

      .card-stats {
        display: flex;
        gap: 20px;
        margin-top: 18px;
        padding-top: 16px;
        border-top: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.06);
      }

      .cstat { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; }
      .cstat-val { font-size: 15px; font-weight: 500; color: var(--ink); }
      .cstat-key { font-size: 11px; color: var(--muted); text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; }

      /* ── INTRO BAND ── */
      .intro-band {
        background: var(--ocean-deep);
        color: white;
        border-radius: 16px;
        padding: 48px 52px;
        margin-bottom: 0;
        display: grid;
        grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
        gap: 48px;
        align-items: center;
      }

      .intro-band h2 {
        font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
        font-size: 32px;
        font-weight: 700;
        line-height: 1.2;
        margin-bottom: 16px;
      }

      .intro-band p {
        font-size: 15px;
        line-height: 1.75;
        color: rgba(255,255,255,0.72);
      }

      .fact-list { list-style: none; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; }
      .fact-list li {
        display: flex;
        align-items: flex-start;
        gap: 12px;
        font-size: 14px;
        line-height: 1.5;
        color: rgba(255,255,255,0.8);
      }

      .fact-dot {
        width: 6px;
        height: 6px;
        border-radius: 50%;
        background: var(--ocean-pale);
        flex-shrink: 0;
        margin-top: 6px;
      }

      /* ── FOOTER ── */
      footer {
        background: var(--ocean-deep);
        color: rgba(255,255,255,0.4);
        text-align: center;
        padding: 28px 40px;
        font-size: 13px;
      }

      footer span { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.7); }

      /* ── RESPONSIVE ── */
      @media (max-width: 700px) {
        .header-inner { padding: 60px 24px 80px; }
        main { padding: 0 24px 60px; }
        .nav-inner { padding: 0 24px; }
        .stat-row { gap: 24px; }
        .intro-band { grid-template-columns: 1fr; padding: 32px 28px; gap: 28px; }
        .shark-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
        footer { padding: 24px; }
      }
    </style>
  </head>
  <body>
    <header>
      <div class="header-bg"></div>
      <svg class="wave-line" viewBox="0 0 1440 60" preserveAspectRatio="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
        <path d="M0,30 C240,60 480,0 720,30 C960,60 1200,0 1440,30 L1440,60 L0,60 Z" fill="#f7f3ee"></path>
      </svg>
      <div class="header-inner">
        <p class="eyebrow">The Ocean's Ancient Rulers</p>
        <h1>Shark<em>pedia</em></h1>
        <p class="header-desc">Sharks have patrolled the world's waters for over 400 million years — long before dinosaurs walked the Earth. Explore the extraordinary diversity of these ancient, essential predators.</p>
        <div class="stat-row">
          <div class="stat">
            <span class="stat-num">500+</span>
            <span class="stat-label">Known species</span>
          </div>
          <div class="stat">
            <span class="stat-num">400M</span>
            <span class="stat-label">Years on Earth</span>
          </div>
          <div class="stat">
            <span class="stat-num">143</span>
            <span class="stat-label">Threatened species</span>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </header>

    <nav>
      <div class="nav-inner">
        <a class="nav-logo" href="#">Sharkpedia</a>
        <div class="nav-divider"></div>
        <div class="nav-links">
          <a class="nav-link" href="#species">Species</a>
          <a class="nav-link" href="#about">About Sharks</a>
        </div>
      </div>
    </nav>

    <main>

      <section id="about" style="padding: 64px 0 48px;">
        <div class="intro-band">
          <div>
            <h2>Masters of adaptation</h2>
            <p>From the colossal, filter-feeding whale shark to the swift and sleek great white, sharks represent a stunning diversity of biology that is often overshadowed by their formidable reputation. Far from being mindless hunters, they are essential engineers of the marine ecosystem, maintaining the delicate balance of life beneath the waves.</p>
          </div>
          <ul class="fact-list">
            <li><span class="fact-dot"></span>Sharks can detect electromagnetic pulses from living prey using specialized organs called ampullae of Lorenzini.</li>
            <li><span class="fact-dot"></span>Their skeleton is made entirely of cartilage — lighter and more flexible than bone.</li>
            <li><span class="fact-dot"></span>Some species, like the Greenland shark, can live for over 400 years, making them the longest-lived vertebrates known.</li>
            <li><span class="fact-dot"></span>Sharks lose and replace tens of thousands of teeth over their lifetime.</li>
            <li><span class="fact-dot"></span>Over a third of all shark species are currently threatened with extinction.</li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </section>

      <section id="species">
        <div class="section-header">
          <span class="section-title">Species profiles</span>
          <div class="section-line"></div>
        </div>

        <div class="shark-grid">

          <!-- Blacktip Reef Shark -->
          <div class="shark-card">
            <div class="card-image-wrap">
              <img src="CarcharhinMelanoptKS.jpg" alt="Image description" /> 
              <span class="status-badge status-vu">Vulnerable</span>
            </div>
            <div class="card-body">
              <p class="card-tag">Carcharhiniformes</p>
              <h2 class="card-name">Blacktip Reef Shark</h2>
              <p class="card-latin">Carcharhinus melanopterus</p>
              <p class="card-desc">One of the most common sharks found in tropical coral reef environments. Easily identified by the prominent black tips on its fins, this species is known for its swift, skittish behavior and preference for shallow lagoons and reef flats.</p>
              <div class="card-stats">
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">1.6 m</span><span class="cstat-key">Avg. length</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">~25 yr</span><span class="cstat-key">Lifespan</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">Indo-Pacific</span><span class="cstat-key">Range</span></div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <!-- Blue Shark -->
          <div class="shark-card">
            <div class="card-image-wrap">
              <img src="Mark-Conlin-NMFS-photo.jpg" alt="Image description" />
              <span class="status-badge status-vu">Near Threatened</span>
            </div>
            <div class="card-body">
              <p class="card-tag">Carcharhiniformes</p>
              <h2 class="card-name">Blue Shark</h2>
              <p class="card-latin">Prionace glauca</p>
              <p class="card-desc">One of the most wide-ranging sharks on Earth, the blue shark is celebrated for its slender build, strikingly vivid blue coloration, and long, graceful pectoral fins. It is highly migratory and can travel thousands of miles across open ocean.</p>
              <div class="card-stats">
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">3.8 m</span><span class="cstat-key">Avg. length</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">~20 yr</span><span class="cstat-key">Lifespan</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">Global</span><span class="cstat-key">Range</span></div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <!-- Hammerhead Shark -->
          <div class="shark-card">
            <div class="card-image-wrap">
              <img src="p0gwb4l3.jpg" alt="Image description" />
               
              <span class="status-badge status-cr">Critically Endangered</span>
            </div>
            <div class="card-body">
              <p class="card-tag">Carcharhiniformes</p>
              <h2 class="card-name">Great Hammerhead</h2>
              <p class="card-latin">Sphyrna mokarran</p>
              <p class="card-desc">The largest of the hammerhead species, instantly recognizable by its distinctive flattened, T-shaped head — called a cephalofoil. This unusual head shape gives the shark superior, 360-degree vision and enhanced electroreception for locating prey buried in the sand.</p>
              <div class="card-stats">
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">6 m</span><span class="cstat-key">Avg. length</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">~44 yr</span><span class="cstat-key">Lifespan</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">Tropical seas</span><span class="cstat-key">Range</span></div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <!-- White Shark -->
          <div class="shark-card">
            <div class="card-image-wrap">
              <img src="AdobeStock_96520936_541522_reduced.jpg" alt="Image description" /> 
              <span class="status-badge status-vu">Vulnerable</span>
            </div>
            <div class="card-body">
              <p class="card-tag">Lamniformes</p>
              <h2 class="card-name">Great White Shark</h2>
              <p class="card-latin">Carcharodon carcharias</p>
              <p class="card-desc">The world's largest predatory fish, the great white is a marvel of evolution. Capable of breaching fully out of the water when attacking prey, it maintains a body temperature warmer than the surrounding ocean — giving it a decisive speed advantage in cold waters.</p>
              <div class="card-stats">
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">6 m</span><span class="cstat-key">Avg. length</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">~70 yr</span><span class="cstat-key">Lifespan</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">Coastal global</span><span class="cstat-key">Range</span></div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <!-- Whale Shark -->
          <div class="shark-card">
            <div class="card-image-wrap">
              <img src="Whale-Shark-Depositphotos-3.3.21.jpg" alt="Image description" />
              <span class="status-badge status-en">Endangered</span>
            </div>
            <div class="card-body">
              <p class="card-tag">Orectolobiformes</p>
              <h2 class="card-name">Whale Shark</h2>
              <p class="card-latin">Rhincodon typus</p>
              <p class="card-desc">The largest fish in the ocean, the whale shark is a gentle giant that feeds exclusively on plankton, small fish, and krill by filter-feeding. Despite its enormous size — up to 12 meters — it poses no threat to humans and is known to interact peacefully with divers.</p>
              <div class="card-stats">
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">12 m</span><span class="cstat-key">Avg. length</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">~130 yr</span><span class="cstat-key">Lifespan</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">Tropical oceans</span><span class="cstat-key">Range</span></div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <!-- Wobbegong -->
          <div class="shark-card">
            <div class="card-image-wrap">
              <img src="iStock-1898249957.jpg" alt="Image description" />
              <span class="status-badge status-vu">Least Concern</span>
            </div>
            <div class="card-body">
              <p class="card-tag">Orectolobiformes</p>
              <h2 class="card-name">Wobbegong</h2>
              <p class="card-latin">Orectolobus maculatus</p>
              <p class="card-desc">A master of camouflage, the wobbegong lies motionless on the ocean floor, perfectly disguised among coral and rock. Its flattened body, ornate skin pattern, and fringe of dermal lobes make it nearly invisible — until prey wanders within striking distance.</p>
              <div class="card-stats">
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">1.5 m</span><span class="cstat-key">Avg. length</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">~25 yr</span><span class="cstat-key">Lifespan</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">Australia</span><span class="cstat-key">Range</span></div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <!-- Basking Shark -->
          <div class="shark-card">
            <div class="card-image-wrap">
              <img src="21745971-1.jpg" alt="Image description" />
              <span class="status-badge status-en">Endangered</span>
            </div>
            <div class="card-body">
              <p class="card-tag">Lamniformes</p>
              <h2 class="card-name">Basking Shark</h2>
              <p class="card-latin">Cetorhinus maximus</p>
              <p class="card-desc">The second-largest fish in the world, the basking shark earns its name by slowly "basking" near the ocean surface, mouth agape, filtering up to 2,000 tons of water per hour through its gill rakers to capture plankton. It is a critically important indicator of marine health.</p>
              <div class="card-stats">
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">10 m</span><span class="cstat-key">Avg. length</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">~50 yr</span><span class="cstat-key">Lifespan</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">Temperate seas</span><span class="cstat-key">Range</span></div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <!-- Shortfin Mako Shark -->
          <div class="shark-card">
            <div class="card-image-wrap">
              <img src="shortfin-mako-Mark-Conlin-SWFSC-Large-Pelagics-Program.jpg" alt="Image description" />
              <span class="status-badge status-en">Endangered</span>
            </div>
            <div class="card-body">
              <p class="card-tag">Lamniformes</p>
              <h2 class="card-name">Shortfin Mako Shark</h2>
              <p class="card-latin">Isurus oxyrinchus</p>
              <p class="card-desc">The shortfin mako is the ocean's fastest shark, reaching hunting speeds of 46 mph. Because it reproduces slowly—with females taking nearly 20 years to mature—overfishing and the fin trade have driven this apex predator to Endangered status, triggering strict international fishing bans to prevent its collapse.</p>
              <div class="card-stats">
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">2.1 m</span><span class="cstat-key">Avg. length</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">~30 yr</span><span class="cstat-key">Lifespan</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">TemperateTropical seas</span><span class="cstat-key">Range</span></div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <!-- Thresher Shark -->
          <div class="shark-card">
            <div class="card-image-wrap">
              <img src="shutterstock-2332746689-huge-licensed-scaled.jpg" alt="Image description" />
              <span class="status-badge status-vu">Vulnerable</span>
            </div>
            <div class="card-body">
              <p class="card-tag">Lamniformes</p>
              <h2 class="card-name">Common Thresher</h2>
              <p class="card-latin">Alopias vulpinus</p>
              <p class="card-desc">Renowned for its extraordinarily long upper tail fin — which can equal the length of its own body — the thresher shark uses this appendage as a whip-like weapon, stunning schools of small fish before consuming them. It is one of the few shark species known to breach.</p>
              <div class="card-stats">
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">5.7 m</span><span class="cstat-key">Avg. length</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">~22 yr</span><span class="cstat-key">Lifespan</span></div>
                <div class="cstat"><span class="cstat-val">Temperate-tropical</span><span class="cstat-key">Range</span></div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>

        </div>
      </section>

    </main>

    <footer>
      <p>Sharkpedia — <span>A field guide to the ocean's oldest predators</span></p>
    </footer>
  </body>
</html>
